content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers LLL and RRR. Find the smallest non-negative integer NNN such that
N % L>N % (L+1)>…>N % (R−1)>N % R .N \,\%\, L \gt N \,\%\, (L + 1) \gt \ldots \gt N \,\%\, (R - 1) \gt N \,\%\, R \,.N%L>N%(L+1)>…>N%(R−1)>N%R.

Here, %\%% is the modulo operator, so A % BA \,\%\, BA%B is the remainder of AAA after division by BBB. For example, 11 % 3=211 \,\%\, 3 = 211%3=2.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the smallest possible NNN, or −1-1−1 if no solution exists.

It is guaranteed that when a solution exists, the smallest solution does not exceed 101810^{18}1018.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: N=6N = 6N=6 satisfies the given condition, since 6 % 4 (=2)>6 % 5 (=1)>6 % 6 (=0)6 \,\%\, 4 \,(= 2) \gt 6 \,\%\, 5 \,(= 1) \gt 6 \,\%\, 6 \,(= 0)6%4(=2)>6%5(=1)>6%6(=0). Notice that N=7N = 7N=7 also satisfies the condition, but it is larger.
Example case 2: It is impossible to find a valid solution because for any non-negative integer NNN, we have N % 1 (=0)≤N % 2N \,\%\, 1 \,(= 0) \le N \,\%\, 2N%1(=0)≤N%2.
